audit_status.source_base: Greek Hebrews text tradition with tabernacle/covenant cultic language; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for first tent, holy places, conscience, blood/covenant, once-for-all offering, appearing.

verses.restored: Now even the first had regulations of service and the earthly holy place.
For a tent was prepared, the first, in which were lampstand and table and setting forth of loaves, which is called Holy Place.
But after the second veil, a tent called Holy of Holies,
having golden altar of incense and ark of covenant covered all around with gold, in which were golden jar having manna, Aaron's rod that budded, and tablets of covenant,
and above it cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy-seat; concerning these things it is not now to speak in detail.
These things being thus prepared, into the first tent the priests go continually, completing the services,
but into the second only the high priest once a year, not without blood, which he offers for himself and for ignorances of the people.
The holy Spirit making this clear: that the way of the holy places had not yet been manifested while the first tent still had standing,
which is a parable for the present season, according to which gifts and sacrifices are offered, not able according to conscience to complete the one serving,
only upon foods and drinks and various washings, regulations of flesh imposed until a season of setting things straight.
But Messiah, having come as high priest of the good things that have come, through the greater and more complete tent not made by hands, that is, not of this creation,
nor through blood of goats and calves but through his own blood/life, entered once for all into the holy places, having found age-long release/redemption.
For if blood of goats and bulls and ashes of heifer sprinkling those made common sanctifies toward fleshly purity,
how much more will the blood/life of Messiah, who through age-long Spirit offered himself blameless to the Divine, cleanse our conscience from dead works to serve living Divine?
And because of this he is mediator of new covenant, so that, death having happened for release of transgressions under the first covenant, those called may receive promise of age-long inheritance.
For where a covenant/testament is, it is necessary for death of the covenant-maker to be borne.
For covenant/testament is firm over dead ones, since it is never strong while the covenant-maker lives.
Therefore not even the first was inaugurated without blood.

For every command according to Law having been spoken by Moses to all the people, taking blood of calves and goats with water and scarlet wool and hyssop, he sprinkled both the scroll itself and all the people,
saying: This is the blood of the covenant that the Divine commanded toward you.
And likewise he sprinkled with blood both the tent and all the vessels of service.
And almost all things are cleansed in blood according to Law, and apart from blood-shedding release does not happen.
Therefore it was necessary for copies of things in heavens to be cleansed by these, but the heavenly things themselves by better sacrifices than these.
For Messiah did not enter holy places made by hands, antitypes of true things, but into heaven itself, now to be manifested before the face of the Divine on our behalf,
nor so that he might offer himself many times, as the high priest enters holy places year by year with blood of another,
since then he would have had to suffer many times from foundation of world; but now once at completion of ages he has been manifested for setting aside sin/misalignment through his sacrifice.
And just as it is laid up for humans once to die, and after this judgment,
so Messiah also, having been offered once to bear sins/misalignments of many, will appear second time apart from sin/misalignment to those eagerly awaiting him, into salvation/healing.

translation_notes: Blood is rendered blood/life where sacrificial life-force is in view, avoiding gore-focused readings.
Conscience cleansing is central: service of living Divine replaces dead works.
Covenant/testament double sense in vv.16-17 is preserved.
safety_note: Cultic contrast must not become contempt for Jewish temple practice. Hebrews interprets access, conscience, and Messiah's heavenly priesthood through tabernacle imagery.
